summary: Jeff Atwood laments the persistent problem of 'tag soup' in web development—the messy intermingling of HTML, JavaScript, and server-side code in view templates. Drawing from his experience building Stack Overflow with ASP.NET MVC, he observes that this problem isn't framework-specific but endemic across all major web stacks including Rails, Django, and ASP.NET. He compares editing such code to playing the game Operation, where one wrong move sets off alarms. The post ends with an open question: is there a better architectural approach, or is increasingly sophisticated tag soup simply the best web development can offer?
key_points: Tag soup—mixing markup and server-side code—is a universal problem across all modern web frameworks, not specific to any one technology
Even 'good' tag soup is inherently difficult to read and maintain because it's hard to see where code ends and markup begins
The problem creates a fragile editing experience where changing HTML risks breaking code and vice versa
Despite framework evolution from classic ASP to Rails to Django to ASP.NET MVC, the fundamental tag soup problem persists
The author genuinely wonders if there's a fundamentally better approach waiting to be discovered
money_quotes: If you're not careful bordering on manically fastidious in constructing your Views, you'll end up with a giant mish-mash of HTML, Javascript, and server-side code.
Now you can double your pleasure: badly formed HTML, meet badly written code.
Do you find this readable? Can you see where the code begins and the markup ends?

Sometimes editing this stuff makes me feel like I'm playing Operation. I have to ever so carefully maneuver my metal tweezers into one tiny slice of code or HTML and make my changes without touching the edges and setting off that blasted electrical buzzer.
The tag soup problem seems to be endemic to all modern web development stacks.
Perhaps when it comes to mixing HTML and server-side code, some form of soup is unavoidable, a necessary evil.
Or is this year's newer, fancier, even-more-delicious iteration of tag soup as good as it ever gets for web development?
key_insight: Despite decades of framework innovation, web development remains fundamentally challenged by the inherent messiness of mixing presentation markup with application logic—a problem that may be intrinsic to the medium itself.
